        return {'display': 'block', 'marginTop': '15px'}
    return {'display': 'none', 'marginTop': '15px'}

# Callback 3: State Dependency - Only update plots on Button Click
@app.callback(
    [Output('box-plot', 'figure'),
     Output('polar-plot', 'figure'),
     Output('status-bar', 'children')],
    Input('run-button', 'n_clicks'),
    [State('particle-dropdown', 'value'),
     State('energy-slider', 'value'),
     State('mode-radio', 'value'),
     State('precision-dropdown', 'value')],
    prevent_initial_call=False
)
def update_visuals(n_clicks, particle, energy_range, mode, precision):
    # Filter data based on states
    filtered_df = df[
        (df['Particle'] == particle) & 
        (df['Energy_MeV'] >= energy_range[0]) & 
        (df['Energy_MeV'] <= energy_range[1])
    ]
    
    # Create Box Plot
    fig_box = px.box(
        filtered_df, 
        y='Energy_MeV', 
        points="all",
        title=f"Energy Distribution for {particle} ({mode} Mode)",
        color_discrete_sequence=['#e74c3c']
    )
    fig_box.update_layout(margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=40, b=40))

    # Create Polar Plot
    fig_polar = px.line_polar(
        filtered_df, 
        r='Momentum', 
        theta='Theta', 
        line_close=True,
        title=f"Angular Momentum Profile: {precision if mode == 'ADV' else 'Standard'}",
        color_discrete_sequence=['#2ecc71']
    )
    fig_polar.update_layout(margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=80, b=40))
    
    status = f"Last Simulation Run: {particle} at Energy {energy_range[0]}-{energy_range[1]} MeV. Mode: {mode}."
    
    return fig_box, fig_polar, status

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State, callback_context
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import dash_daq as daq

# Set seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

# Generate dummy experimental data for Enzyme Kinetics
# Substrate concentration [S] (uM) vs Velocity v (umol/min)
S_exp = np.array([1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 40, 60, 100])
V_max_true = 120
Km_true = 15
# Michaelis-Menten equation with some noise
v_exp = (V_max_true * S_exp) / (Km_true + S_exp) + np.random.normal(0, 2, len(S_exp))
df_exp = pd.DataFrame({'Substrate': S_exp, 'Velocity': v_exp})

app = dash.Dash(__name__)

app.layout = html.Div([
    html.Div([
        html.H2("Enzyme Kinetics Simulator: Michaelis-Menten Analysis", style={'textAlign': 'center', 'marginBottom': '10px'}),
        dcc.Markdown("""
            Adjust the maximum velocity ($V_{max}$) and Michaelis constant ($K_m$) to fit the theoretical curve to the experimental data. 
            The **Substrate** and **Saturation** inputs are synchronized.
        """, style={'textAlign': 'center', 'fontSize': '14px', 'color': '#555'})
    ], style={'padding': '10px', 'backgroundColor': '#f9f9f9', 'borderBottom': '1px solid #ddd'}),

    html.Div([
        # Control Panel
        html.Div([
            html.Label("Model Preset (Chained Callback)", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
            dcc.RadioItems(
                id='preset-selector',
                options=[
                    {'label': 'Wild Type', 'value': 'wt'},
                    {'label': 'Mutant A', 'value': 'mut_a'},
                    {'label': 'Inhibited', 'value': 'inh'}
                ],
                value='wt',
                style={'marginBottom': '20px'}
            ),

            html.Label("V-max (Max Velocity):", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
            dcc.Slider(id='vmax-slider', min=50, max=200, step=5, value=120, 
                       marks={50: '50', 120: '120', 200: '200'}),
            
            html.Label("K-m (Binding Affinity):", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
            dcc.Slider(id='km-slider', min=1, max=50, step=1, value=15,
                       marks={1: '1', 15: '15', 50: '50'}),

            html.Hr(),
            html.Label("Test Concentration ([S] uM) vs Saturation (%) - Circular Logic", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
            html.Div([
                html.Div([
                    html.Label("Substrate [S]"),
                    dcc.Input(id='substrate-input', type='number', value=15, style={'width': '80%'}),
                ], style={'width': '48%', 'display': 'inline-block'}),
                html.Div([
                    html.Label("Saturation %"),
                    dcc.Input(id='saturation-input', type='number', value=50, style={'width': '80%'}),
                ], style={'width': '48%', 'display': 'inline-block'}),
            ]),
            
            html.Div([
                daq.Gauge(
                    id='saturation-gauge',
                    label="Current Saturation",
                    value=50,
                    max=100,
                    min=0,
                    size=160,
                    color={"gradient":True,"ranges":{"green":[0,60],"yellow":[60,80],"red":[80,100]}}
                )
            ], style={'marginTop': '20px', 'textAlign': 'center'})

        ], style={'width': '30%', 'padding': '20px', 'borderRight': '1px solid #ddd'}),

        # Visualization Panel
        html.Div([
            html.Div([
                dcc.Graph(id='kinetics-plot', style={'height': '400px'})
            ]),
            html.Div([
                dcc.Graph(id='error-box-plot', style={'height': '350px'})
            ])
        ], style={'width': '68%', 'display': 'inline-block', 'verticalAlign': 'top'})
    ], style={'display': 'flex', 'flexDirection': 'row', 'height': '850px'})
], style={'fontFamily': 'sans-serif', 'maxWidth': '1920px', 'margin': '0 auto'})

# 1. Chained Callbacks: Preset updates Sliders
@app.callback(
    [Output('vmax-slider', 'value'),
     Output('km-slider', 'value')],
    [Input('preset-selector', 'value')]
)
def update_presets(preset):
    if preset == 'wt':
        return 120, 15
    elif preset == 'mut_a':
        return 180, 40
    else: # inhibited
        return 120, 45

# 2. Circular Callbacks: Substrate <-> Saturation
# Saturation % = ([S] / (Km + [S])) * 100
@app.callback(
    [Output('substrate-input', 'value'),
     Output('saturation-input', 'value'),
     Output('saturation-gauge', 'value')],
    [Input('substrate-input', 'value'),
     Input('saturation-input', 'value')],
    [State('km-slider', 'value')]
)
def sync_inputs(s_val, sat_val, km):
    ctx = callback_context
    if not ctx.triggered:
        return s_val, sat_val, sat_val
    
    trigger_id = ctx.triggered[0]['prop_id'].split('.')[0]
    
    try:
        if trigger_id == 'substrate-input':
            # Update saturation based on S
            new_sat = (s_val / (km + s_val)) * 100
            return s_val, round(new_sat, 1), round(new_sat, 1)
        else:
            # Update substrate based on Saturation
            # [S] = (Sat * Km) / (100 - Sat)
            if sat_val >= 100: sat_val = 99.9
            if sat_val <= 0: sat_val = 0.1
            new_s = (sat_val * km) / (100 - sat_val)
            return round(new_s, 2), sat_val, sat_val
    except:
        return s_val, sat_val, sat_val

# 3. Visualization update (State Dependency)
@app.callback(
    [Output('kinetics-plot', 'figure'),
     Output('error-box-plot', 'figure')],
    [Input('vmax-slider', 'value'),
     Input('km-slider', 'value'),
     Input('substrate-input', 'value')]
)
def update_graphs(vmax, km, s_highlight):
    # Theoretical curve
    s_model = np.linspace(0, 110, 50)
    v_model = (vmax * s_model) / (km + s_model)
    df_model = pd.DataFrame({'Substrate': s_model, 'Velocity': v_model, 'Type': 'Model'})
    
    # Visualizing current model vs experimental
    fig_kinetics = px.area(df_model, x='Substrate', y='Velocity', 
                          title=f"Reaction Velocity (Vmax={vmax}, Km={km})",
                          labels={'Velocity': 'v (umol/min)', 'Substrate': '[S] (uM)'},
                          color_discrete_sequence=['#cbd5e8'])
    
    # Add experimental points
    fig_kinetics.add_scatter(x=df_exp['Substrate'], y=df_exp['Velocity'], 
                            mode='markers', name='Experimental Data',
                            marker=dict(color='red', size=8))
    
    # Add highlight for current substrate concentration
    v_highlight = (vmax * s_highlight) / (km + s_highlight)
    fig_kinetics.add_scatter(x=[s_highlight], y=[v_highlight], 
                            mode='markers', name='Current [S]',
                            marker=dict(color='black', size=12, symbol='x'))

    # Calculate residuals (State/Input dependency logic)
    # Predicted v for experimental [S]
    v_pred = (vmax * df_exp['Substrate']) / (km + df_exp['Substrate'])
    residuals = df_exp['Velocity'] - v_pred
    df_res = pd.DataFrame({'Residuals': residuals, 'Experiment': 'Data'})
    
    fig_box = px.box(df_res, y='Residuals', points="all", title="Model Error Distribution (Residuals)",
                    color_discrete_sequence=['#7fc97f'])
    fig_box.add_hline(y=0, line_dash="dash", line_color="black")
    
    return fig_kinetics, fig_box

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State, callback_context
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Seed for reproducibility in initial data generation
np.random.seed(42)

app = dash.Dash(__name__)

# --- Helper Functions for Data Generation ---

def generate_landscape(func_type, bounds, res=50):
    """Generates the Z-matrix for the heatmap based on function type."""
    x = np.linspace(bounds[0], bounds[1], res)
    y = np.linspace(bounds[0], bounds[1], res)
    X, Y = np.meshgrid(x, y)
    
    if func_type == 'Basin':
        # Simple quadratic bowl
        Z = X**2 + Y**2
    else: # 'Ripples' (Rastrigin-like)
        # Multimodal surface
        Z = X**2 + Y**2 - 10 * (np.cos(2 * np.pi * X) + np.cos(2 * np.pi * Y))
        
    return X, Y, Z

def init_particles(n, bounds):
    """Initialize particles uniformly within bounds."""
    # We use a local random state to ensure some variance while keeping the global seed fixed
    return pd.DataFrame({
        'x': np.random.uniform(bounds[0], bounds[1], n),
        'y': np.random.uniform(bounds[0], bounds[1], n),
        'id': range(n)
    })

def update_particles(df, func_type):
    """Move particles towards the minimum (0,0) with noise."""
    # Simulation logic: Particles drift towards center (global min)
    # Different physics for different landscapes
    
    if func_type == 'Basin':
        # Fast convergence
        decay = 0.8
        noise_scale = 0.5
    else:
        # Slower convergence due to 'local minima' friction
        decay = 0.95
        noise_scale = 1.5

    # Move towards 0,0
    df['x'] = df['x'] * decay + np.random.normal(0, noise_scale * 0.1, len(df))
    df['y'] = df['y'] * decay + np.random.normal(0, noise_scale * 0.1, len(df))
    
    return df

# --- Layout ---

app.layout = html.Div(style={'fontFamily': 'sans-serif', 'height': '100vh', 'display': 'flex', 'flexDirection': 'column', 'overflow': 'hidden'}, children=[
    
    # Header
    html.Div(style={'backgroundColor': '#2c3e50', 'color': 'white', 'padding': '15px'}, children=[
        html.H2("Stochastic Optimization Visualizer", style={'margin': 0}),
        html.P("Simulating particle convergence on 2D objective functions.", style={'margin': 0, 'fontSize': '14px', 'opacity': '0.8'})
    ]),

    # Main Content Area
    html.Div(style={'display': 'flex', 'flex': '1', 'overflow': 'hidden'}, children=[
        
        # Sidebar Controls
        html.Div(style={'width': '300px', 'backgroundColor': '#f8f9fa', 'padding': '20px', 'borderRight': '1px solid #ddd', 'display': 'flex', 'flexDirection': 'column', 'gap': '20px'}, children=[
            
            html.Div([
                html.Label("1. Select Objective Function:", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
                dcc.RadioItems(
                    id='radio-function',
                    options=['Basin', 'Ripples'],
                    value='Basin',
                    labelStyle={'display': 'block', 'marginTop': '5px'}
                )
            ]),

            html.Div([
                html.Label("2. Initialization Bounds:", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
                html.Div(id='slider-output-container', style={'marginBottom': '5px', 'fontSize': '12px', 'color': '#666'}),
                dcc.RangeSlider(
                    id='slider-bounds',
                    min=-10, max=10, step=1,
                    value=[-5, 5],
                    marks={-10: '-10', -5: '-5', 0: '0', 5: '5', 10: '10'}
                )
            ]),
            
            html.Div(style={'borderTop': '1px solid #ddd', 'margin': '10px 0'}),

            html.Div([
                html.Label("3. Simulation Control:", style={'fontWeight': 'bold'}),
                html.Div(style={'display': 'flex', 'gap': '10px', 'marginTop': '10px'}, children=[
                    html.Button('Iterate Step', id='btn-iterate', n_clicks=0, style={'flex': '1', 'backgroundColor': '#007bff', 'color': 'white', 'border': 'none', 'padding': '10px', 'cursor': 'pointer'}),
                    html.Button('Reset', id='btn-reset', n_clicks=0, style={'flex': '1', 'backgroundColor': '#6c757d', 'color': 'white', 'border': 'none', 'padding': '10px', 'cursor': 'pointer'})
                ])
            ]),

            html.Div(children=[
                html.Strong("Iteration: "),
                html.Span(id='iteration-display', children="0")
            ], style={'marginTop': 'auto', 'padding': '10px', 'backgroundColor': '#e9ecef', 'borderRadius': '5px'})
        ]),

        # Charts Area
        html.Div(style={'flex': '1', 'padding': '20px', 'display': 'flex', 'flexDirection': 'row', 'gap': '20px'}, children=[
            
            # Left: Landscape Heatmap
            html.Div(style={'flex': '1', 'display': 'flex', 'flexDirection': 'column'}, children=[
                html.H4("Objective Landscape", style={'textAlign': 'center'}),
                dcc.Graph(id='graph-landscape', style={'flex': '1'})
            ]),

            # Right: Particle Scatter
            html.Div(style={'flex': '1', 'display': 'flex', 'flexDirection': 'column'}, children=[
                html.H4("Particle Swarm State", style={'textAlign': 'center'}),
                dcc.Graph(id='graph-particles', style={'flex': '1'})
            ])
        ])
    ]),

    # Stores
    dcc.Store(id='store-particles'), # Stores the dataframe as JSON
    dcc.Store(id='store-iteration', data=0)
])

# --- Callbacks ---

# 1. Chained Callback: Function Selection -> Updates Slider Bounds
@app.callback(
    [Output('slider-bounds', 'min'),
     Output('slider-bounds', 'max'),
     Output('slider-bounds', 'value'),
     Output('slider-bounds', 'marks')],
    [Input('radio-function', 'value')]
)
def update_slider_constraints(func_type):
    if func_type == 'Ripples':
        # Ripples needs a wider area to show multimodal nature
        return -10, 10, [-8, 8], {-10: '-10', -8: '-8', 0: '0', 8: '8', 10: '10'}
    else:
        # Basin is simpler, focus on center
        return -5, 5, [-4, 4], {-5: '-5', -4: '-4', 0: '0', 4: '4', 5: '5'}

# 2. State Management: Handles Reset and Iteration
@app.callback(
    [Output('store-particles', 'data'),
     Output('store-iteration', 'data')],
    [Input('btn-iterate', 'n_clicks'),
     Input('btn-reset', 'n_clicks'),
     Input('radio-function', 'value')], # Changing function auto-resets
    [State('slider-bounds', 'value'),
     State('store-particles', 'data'),
     State('store-iteration', 'data')]
)
def manage_simulation_state(btn_iter, btn_reset, func_type, bounds, current_data, current_iter):
    ctx = callback_context
    trigger_id = ctx.triggered[0]['prop_id'].split('.')[0]

    # Initialize or Reset condition
    # Triggers: Page load (no trigger), Reset button, Function change, or missing data
    if not ctx.triggered or trigger_id == 'btn-reset' or trigger_id == 'radio-function' or current_data is None:
        # Generate fresh particles
        df = init_particles(20, bounds)
        return df.to_json(orient='split', date_format='iso'), 0

    # Iteration condition
    if trigger_id == 'btn-iterate':
        df = pd.read_json(current_data, orient='split')
        df = update_particles(df, func_type)
        return df.to_json(orient='split', date_format='iso'), current_iter + 1
    
    return current_data, current_iter

# 3. Visualization: Renders charts based on State
@app.callback(
    [Output('graph-landscape', 'figure'),
     Output('graph-particles', 'figure'),
     Output('iteration-display', 'children')],
    [Input('store-particles', 'data'),
     Input('store-iteration', 'data')],
    [State('radio-function', 'value')] # We need to know which function to draw
)
def update_charts(particles_json, iteration, func_type):
    if not particles_json:
        return dash.no_update, dash.no_update, "0"
    
    df = pd.read_json(particles_json, orient='split')
    
    # --- Chart 1: Heatmap (Landscape) ---
    # Determine view range based on function type for static background
    bound_limit = 10 if func_type == 'Ripples' else 5
    X, Y, Z = generate_landscape(func_type, [-bound_limit, bound_limit])
    
    fig_landscape = px.imshow(
        Z, 
        x=np.linspace(-bound_limit, bound_limit, 50), 
        y=np.linspace(-bound_limit, bound_limit, 50),
        color_continuous_scale='Viridis',
        origin='lower'
    )
    fig_landscape.update_layout(
        margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=30, b=20),
        title_text=f"{func_type} Topography"
    )

    # --- Chart 2: Scatter (Particles) ---
    # Calculate distance from center for color
    df['dist'] = np.sqrt(df['x']**2 + df['y']**2)
    
    fig_particles = px.scatter(
        df, x='x', y='y', 
        color='dist',
        size_max=15,
        range_x=[-bound_limit, bound_limit],
        range_y=[-bound_limit, bound_limit],
        color_continuous_scale='Magma_r'
    )
    fig_particles.update_traces(marker=dict(size=12, line=dict(width=1, color='DarkSlateGrey')))
    fig_particles.update_layout(
        margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=30, b=20),
        title_text=f"Particle Positions (n={len(df)})"
    )

    return fig_landscape, fig_particles, str(iteration)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State
import dash_bootstrap_components as dbc
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Data Generation (Self-contained, Seeded)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
np.random.seed(42)

def generate_marketing_data():
    # Trends and Categories
    channels = ['Email', 'Social Media', 'PPC']
    # Restrict regions per channel to force dependent logic
    channel_region_map = {
        'Email': ['North America', 'Europe'],
        'Social Media': ['Asia', 'Europe', 'South America'],
        'PPC': ['North America', 'Asia']
    }
    
    data = []
    dates = pd.date_range(start="2023-01-01", periods=12, freq="M")
    
    for channel in channels:
        for region in channel_region_map[channel]:
            # Base performance
            base_sales = np.random.randint(2000, 5000)
            growth_factor = 1.05 if channel == 'Social Media' else 0.98
            
            for i, date in enumerate(dates):
                # Add some trend and seasonality
                sales = int(base_sales * (growth_factor ** i) + np.random.normal(0, 200))
                roi = round(np.random.uniform(1.5, 4.0), 2)
                
                # Customer metrics for Scatter Matrix
                # Generate 5 customer clusters per month/region aggregate
                for _ in range(5): 
                    cust_age = int(np.random.normal(35, 10))
                    cust_spend = int(sales / 50 + np.random.normal(0, 50))
                    cust_loyalty = round(np.random.uniform(1, 10), 1)
                    
                    data.append({
                        'Date': date,
                        'Channel': channel,
                        'Region': region,
                        'Sales': sales,
                        'ROI': roi,
                        'Customer Age': max(18, min(80, cust_age)),
                        'Avg Spend': max(10, cust_spend),
                        'Loyalty Score': cust_loyalty
                    })

    return pd.DataFrame(data), channel_region_map

df, CHANNEL_REGION_MAP = generate_marketing_data()

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. App Configuration & Layout
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
app = dash.Dash(__name__, external_stylesheets=[dbc.themes.LUX])
app.title = "Marketing Analytics Hub"

app.layout = dbc.Container([
    # Header
    dbc.Row([
        dbc.Col(html.H2("Campaign ROI & Customer Segmentation", className="text-primary"), width=12)
    ], className="mb-4 mt-4"),

    dbc.Row([
        # --- Sidebar (Controls) ---
        dbc.Col([
            dbc.Card([
                dbc.CardHeader("Campaign Settings"),
                dbc.CardBody([
                    # Control 1: Channel (Master)
                    html.Label("1. Select Channel:", className="fw-bold"),
                    dcc.Dropdown(
                        id='channel-dropdown',
                        options=[{'label': c, 'value': c} for c in CHANNEL_REGION_MAP.keys()],
                        value='Email',
                        clearable=False
                    ),
                    html.Br(),

                    # Control 2: Region (Dependent/Chained)
                    html.Label("2. Target Region:", className="fw-bold"),
                    dcc.Dropdown(
                        id='region-dropdown',
                        options=[], # Populated by callback
                        value=None,
                        placeholder="Select Region..."
                    ),
                    html.Br(),

                    # Control 3: Budget Simulator (Hidden in Accordion)
                    dbc.Accordion([
                        dbc.AccordionItem([
                            html.Label("Ad Spend Multiplier"),
                            dcc.Slider(
                                id='budget-slider',
                                min=0.5, max=2.0, step=0.1, value=1.0,
                                marks={0.5: 'Low', 1.0: 'Normal', 2.0: 'Double'},
                                tooltip={"placement": "bottom", "always_visible": True}
                            )
                        ], title="Advanced Budgeting")
                    ], start_collapsed=True),
                    
                    html.Hr(),
                    
                    # Action Button (State Trigger)
                    dbc.Button(
                        "Run Analysis", 
                        id='run-btn', 
                        color="primary", 
                        className="w-100",
                        n_clicks=0
                    )
                ])
            ], className="h-100 shadow-sm")
        ], width=3),

        # --- Main Content (Charts) ---
        dbc.Col([
            # KPI Row
            dbc.Row([
                dbc.Col(dbc.Alert(id='kpi-sales', color="info"), width=6),
                dbc.Col(dbc.Alert(id='kpi-roi', color="success"), width=6),
            ], className="mb-3"),

            # Charts Row
            dbc.Row([
                dbc.Col([
                    dbc.Card([
                        dbc.CardHeader("Revenue Trend (Simulated)"),
                        dcc.Loading(dcc.Graph(id='line-chart', style={'height': '350px'}))
                    ])
                ], width=6),
                dbc.Col([
                    dbc.Card([
                        dbc.CardHeader("Customer Segmentation Matrix"),
                        dcc.Loading(dcc.Graph(id='scatter-matrix', style={'height': '350px'}))
                    ])
                ], width=6)
            ])
        ], width=9)
    ])
], fluid=True, style={'height': '100vh', 'overflow': 'hidden'})

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Callbacks (Complexity Level 3)
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Callback 1: Chained Callback (Input -> Input)
# Updating Channel updates available Regions and resets Region value
@app.callback(
    [Output('region-dropdown', 'options'),
     Output('region-dropdown', 'value')],
    Input('channel-dropdown', 'value')
)
def update_region_options(selected_channel):
    available_regions = CHANNEL_REGION_MAP.get(selected_channel, [])
    options = [{'label': r, 'value': r} for r in available_regions]
    # Default to the first region in the new list to avoid null states
    default_val = available_regions[0] if available_regions else None
    return options, default_val

# Callback 2: State Dependency (Input + State -> Output)
# Charts only update when Button is clicked, reading the State of dropdowns/slider
@app.callback(
    [Output('line-chart', 'figure'),
     Output('scatter-matrix', 'figure'),
     Output('kpi-sales', 'children'),
     Output('kpi-roi', 'children')],
    Input('run-btn', 'n_clicks'),
    [State('channel-dropdown', 'value'),
     State('region-dropdown', 'value'),
     State('budget-slider', 'value')]
)
def update_dashboard(n_clicks, channel, region, budget_multiplier):
    # Filter Data
    filtered_df = df[(df['Channel'] == channel) & (df['Region'] == region)].copy()
    
    if filtered_df.empty:
        # Fallback for initialization or empty states
        empty_fig = px.scatter(title="No Data Selected")
        return empty_fig, empty_fig, "N/A", "N/A"

    # Apply Simulation Logic (State-based manipulation)
    # The budget slider simulates an increase in sales but a decrease in ROI initially
    filtered_df['Simulated Sales'] = filtered_df['Sales'] * budget_multiplier
    filtered_df['Simulated ROI'] = filtered_df['ROI'] * (1 / (budget_multiplier ** 0.5))

    # Aggregating for Line Chart
    daily_agg = filtered_df.groupby('Date')[['Simulated Sales', 'Simulated ROI']].mean().reset_index()

    # 1. Line Chart
    fig_line = px.line(
        daily_agg, 
        x='Date', 
        y='Simulated Sales',
        title=f"Proj. Revenue: {channel} ({region})",
        markers=True
    )
    fig_line.update_layout(margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=40, b=20), paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)")

    # 2. Scatter Matrix (Customer Segmentation)
    # Sampling to keep it clean visually, showing relation between Age, Spend, Loyalty
    fig_matrix = px.scatter_matrix(
        filtered_df,
        dimensions=['Customer Age', 'Avg Spend', 'Loyalty Score'],
        color="Simulated ROI",
        title="Customer Cluster Analysis"
    )
    fig_matrix.update_layout(margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=40, b=20), paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)")
    fig_matrix.update_traces(diagonal_visible=False)

    # 3. KPI Updates
    total_sales = filtered_df['Simulated Sales'].sum()
    avg_roi = filtered_df['Simulated ROI'].mean()
    
    kpi_sales_text = f"Total Projected Sales: ${total_sales:,.0f}"
    kpi_roi_text = f"Avg ROI: {avg_roi:.2f}x"

    return fig_line, fig_matrix, kpi_sales_text, kpi_roi_text

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

    ])

], fluid=True)

# 4. Callbacks

# Logic 1: Chained Callback (Region -> Segment Options)
# When Region changes, update the Checklist options and default selection
@app.callback(
    [Output('segment-checklist', 'options'),
     Output('segment-checklist', 'value')],
    Input('region-dropdown', 'value')
)
def update_segments(selected_region):
    relevant_segments = segments_map.get(selected_region, [])
    options = [{'label': s, 'value': s} for s in relevant_segments]
    # Default select all
    return options, relevant_segments

# Logic 2: Conditional Visibility (Campaign Type -> Discount Input)
# If 'Clearance Sale' is selected, show the Discount input field
@app.callback(
    Output('discount-wrapper', 'style'),
    Input('campaign-type', 'value')
)
def toggle_discount_input(campaign):
    if campaign == 'clearance':
        return {'display': 'block'}
    return {'display': 'none'}

# Logic 3: State Dependency (Button + Inputs -> Charts)
# The charts only update when "Simulate ROI" is clicked, using the State of other inputs
@app.callback(
    [Output('roi-chart', 'figure'),
     Output('profile-chart', 'figure')],
    [Input('simulate-btn', 'n_clicks'),
     Input('region-dropdown', 'value')], # Also trigger on Region change to avoid blank initial state
    [State('segment-checklist', 'value'),
     State('budget-input', 'value'),
     State('campaign-type', 'value'),
     State('discount-input', 'value')]
)
def update_charts(n_clicks, region_trig, selected_segments, budget, campaign, discount):
    # Filter Data
    # Note: On initial load, selected_segments might be None or stale if triggered by Region,
    # so we fallback to all segments in that region if list is empty.
    
    ctx_id = ctx.triggered_id if ctx.triggered else 'region-dropdown'
    
    filtered_df = df[df['Region'] == region_trig].copy()
    
    # If segments provided, filter further
    if selected_segments:
        filtered_df = filtered_df[filtered_df['Segment'].isin(selected_segments)]
    
    if filtered_df.empty:
        # Return empty figures if no data
        return px.bar(title="No Data"), px.scatter(title="No Data")

    # Simulation Logic
    # Adjust ROI based on Campaign and Inputs
    multiplier = 1.0
    if campaign == 'vip':
        multiplier = 1.2
    elif campaign == 'clearance':
        # Clearance might have lower margin but higher volume (simulated here simply)
        disc_factor = (100 - (discount or 0)) / 100
        multiplier = 0.8 * disc_factor
    
    # Calculate Simulated Revenue
    # Revenue = (Budget / count) * ROI_Base * Multiplier * (Monetary/1000)
    filtered_df['Simulated_Revenue'] = (budget / len(filtered_df)) * filtered_df['ROI_Base'] * multiplier * (filtered_df['Monetary'] / 500)
    
    # Chart 1: Bar Chart
    fig_roi = px.bar(
        filtered_df.groupby('Segment')['Simulated_Revenue'].sum().reset_index(),
        x='Segment', 
        y='Simulated_Revenue',
        title=f"Projected Revenue ({campaign.title()} Strategy)",
        color='Segment',
        template='plotly_white'
    )
    
    # Chart 2: Parallel Coordinates
    # Selecting numerical columns for analysis
    fig_profile = px.parallel_coordinates(
        filtered_df,
        dimensions=['Recency', 'Frequency', 'Monetary'],
        color='Monetary',
        title="Customer Behavior Profile",
        color_continuous_scale=px.colors.diverging.Tealrose
    )
    
    return fig_roi, fig_profile

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import numpy as np

# Initialize the application
app = dash.Dash(__name__)
app.title = "Portfolio Stress Test Simulator"

# --- Constants & Helper Functions ---
REGIONS = ['North America', 'Europe', 'Asia Pacific']
PRESETS = ['Conservative', 'Balanced', 'Aggressive']

def generate_simulation_data(region, equity_pct, investment_amount):
    """
    Generates dummy financial data based on inputs.
    """
    np.random.seed(42)  # Fixed seed for reproducibility
    
    # Coefficients based on inputs
    bond_pct = 100 - equity_pct
    
    # Region multipliers (Growth, Volatility)
    region_factors = {
        'North America': (1.05, 0.15),
        'Europe': (1.03, 0.10),
        'Asia Pacific': (1.07, 0.20)
    }
    growth_base, vol_base = region_factors.get(region, (1.04, 0.12))
    
    # Portfolio weighted stats
    # Equity: High growth, High vol. Bond: Low growth, Low vol.
    weighted_growth = (equity_pct * 1.08 + bond_pct * 1.02) / 100 * growth_base
    weighted_vol = (equity_pct * 0.25 + bond_pct * 0.05) / 100 * vol_base
    
    # 1. Bar Chart Data: 12-Month Projection
    months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun', 
              'Jul', 'Aug', 'Sep', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec']
    
    balance = []
    current_val = float(investment_amount)
    for _ in months:
        # Random monthly return
        monthly_return = np.random.normal(weighted_growth/12, weighted_vol/np.sqrt(12))
        current_val *= (1 + monthly_return)
        balance.append(current_val)
        
    df_proj = pd.DataFrame({
        'Month': months,
        'Projected Balance': balance,
        'Type': ['Projection'] * 12
    })
    
    # 2. Polar Chart Data: Risk Profile Metrics
    # Normalize metrics to 0-10 scale
    metrics = {
        'Volatility': min(10, weighted_vol * 100 * 2),
        'Exp. Return': min(10, (weighted_growth - 1) * 100 * 1.5),
        'Liquidity': 8 if equity_pct > 50 else 9, # Arbitrary logic
        'Diversification': 10 - abs(50 - equity_pct)/10, # Peak at 50/50
        'ESG Score': 6 + (bond_pct/100 * 2) # Bonds slightly better ESG in this dummy model
    }
    
    df_risk = pd.DataFrame({
        'Metric': list(metrics.keys()),
        'Score': list(metrics.values())
    })
    
    return df_proj, df_risk

# --- Layout ---

                        # Bottom Chart: Polar
                        html.Div(

)

# --- Callbacks ---

# 1. Chained Callback: Update Slider based on Tab Preset
@app.callback(
    Output('equity-slider', 'value'),
    Input('strategy-tabs', 'value')
)
def update_slider_preset(strategy):
    if strategy == 'Conservative':
        return 20
    elif strategy == 'Aggressive':
        return 80
    return 50  # Balanced

# 2. UI Helper: Display Slider Value
@app.callback(

)
def update_label(value):
    return f"Current Setting: {value}% Equity / {100-value}% Bonds"

# 3. State-Dependent Callback: Update Charts on Button Click
@app.callback(
    [Output('balance-bar-chart', 'figure'),
     Output('risk-polar-chart', 'figure')],
    [Input('run-btn', 'n_clicks')],
    [State('region-dropdown', 'value'),
     State('equity-slider', 'value'),
     State('investment-input', 'value')]
)
def update_graphs(n_clicks, region, equity_pct, investment):
    # Default trigger logic handled by n_clicks or initialization
    # If None inputs (startup), provide defaults
    if region is None: region = 'North America'
    if equity_pct is None: equity_pct = 50
    if investment is None: investment = 10000
    
    df_proj, df_risk = generate_simulation_data(region, equity_pct, investment)
    
    # Bar Chart: Projected Balance
    fig_bar = px.bar(
        df_proj, 
        x='Month', 
        y='Projected Balance',
        title=f"12-Month Projected Growth ({region})",
        color_discrete_sequence=['#3498db']
    )
    fig_bar.update_layout(margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=40, b=40), paper_bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
    
    # Polar Chart: Risk Metrics
    fig_polar = px.line_polar(
        df_risk, 
        r='Score', 
        theta='Metric', 
        line_close=True,
        range_r=[0, 10],
        title="Risk & Performance Profile",
        color_discrete_sequence=['#e74c3c']
    )
    fig_polar.update_traces(fill='toself')
    fig_polar.update_layout(margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=40, b=40), paper_bgcolor='rgba(0,0,0,0)')
    
    return fig_bar, fig_polar

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State, callback_context
import dash_daq as daq
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Fixed random seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

# Generate dummy financial dataset
# Micro-narrative: "Analyzing quarterly revenue trends across three service sectors
# with dynamic breakdown by region and fraud alert toggle impacting filters."

# Dimensions:
# - Sectors: Consulting, Cloud Services, Marketing
# - Regions: North America, Europe, Asia, LATAM
# - Quarters: Q1-Q4 for 3 years (2019-2021)
# - Revenue generated with trends and periodic seasonality + noise
# - Fraud Alert flag simulated at sector-region-year level

sectors = ['Consulting', 'Cloud Services', 'Marketing']
regions = ['North America', 'Europe', 'Asia', 'LATAM']
years = [2019, 2020, 2021]
quarters = ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4']

# Build DataFrame with all combinations
records = []
for sector in sectors:
    base = {'Consulting': 100, 'Cloud Services': 80, 'Marketing': 60}[sector]
    trend = {'Consulting': 1.05, 'Cloud Services': 1.10, 'Marketing': 1.07}[sector]
    seasonal = [1.0, 1.2, 0.9, 1.1]  # Q1-Q4 pattern repeated
    for region in regions:
        # Regional modifier
        region_mod = {'North America': 1.3, 'Europe': 1.1, 'Asia': 0.9, 'LATAM': 0.7}[region]
        # Fraud alert simulated randomly per sector-region-year
        for year in years:
            fraud_alert = np.random.choice([True, False], p=[0.15, 0.85])
            for i, qtr in enumerate(quarters):
                # Revenue calculation with trend, seasonal effect, region mod and random noise
                quarter_index = (year - 2019)*4 + i
                rev = (
                    base * (trend ** (year - 2019)) * seasonal[i] * region_mod +
                    np.random.normal(0, 5)
                )
                records.append({
                    'Sector': sector,
                    'Region': region,
                    'Year': year,
                    'Quarter': qtr,
                    'Quarter_Num': quarter_index,
                    'Revenue': max(0, round(rev, 2)),
                    'FraudAlert': fraud_alert
                })

df = pd.DataFrame(records)

# Precompute quarters as a combined label for slider ticks
quarters_labels = [f"{y} {q}" for y in years for q in quarters]

app = dash.Dash(__name__)

app.layout = html.Div(
    style={'width': '90%', 'margin': 'auto', 'font-family': 'Arial, sans-serif', 'padding-top': '15px'},
    children=[
        html.H2("Quarterly Revenue Dashboard: Sector & Region Analysis"),

        html.Div(
            style={'display': 'flex', 'justify-content': 'space-between', 'margin-bottom': '10px'},
            children=[
                html.Div(
                    [
                        html.Label("Select Sector:"),
                        dcc.Dropdown(
                            id='sector-dropdown',
                            options=[{'label': s, 'value': s} for s in sectors],
                            value='Consulting',
                            clearable=False,
                            style={'width': '220px'}
                        ),
                    ]
                ),

                html.Div(
                    [
                        html.Label("Select Region(s):"),
                        dcc.Dropdown(
                            id='region-dropdown',
                            options=[{'label': r, 'value': r} for r in regions],
                            value=['North America'],
                            multi=True,
                            clearable=False,
                            style={'width': '300px'}
                        ),
                    ]
                ),

                html.Div(
                    [
                        html.Label("Fraud Alert Filter:"),
                        daq.BooleanSwitch(
                            id='fraud-toggle',
                            on=False,
                            label='Show Fraud-only',
                            labelPosition='top'
                        ),
                    ],
                    style={'width': '160px', 'textAlign': 'center'}
                ),
            ]
        ),

        html.Div(
            [
                html.Label("Select Date Range (Quarters):"),
                dcc.RangeSlider(
                    id='quarter-slider',
                    min=0,
                    max=len(quarters_labels) - 1,
                    value=[0, len(quarters_labels)-1],
                    marks={i: lbl for i, lbl in enumerate(quarters_labels)},
                    allowCross=False,
                    tooltip={'placement': 'bottom'}
                ),
            ],
            style={'margin-bottom': '20px'}
        ),

        html.Div(
            [
                # Conditional subregion checklists appear only if multiple regions selected AND fraud toggle ON
                html.Div(
                    [
                        html.Label("Sub-region Filter:"),
                        dcc.Checklist(
                            id='subregion-checklist',
                            options=[],  # populated dynamically
                            value=[],
                            labelStyle={'display': 'inline-block', 'marginRight': '15px'}
                        )
                    ],
                    id='subregion-container',
                    style={'display': 'none', 'marginBottom': '20px'}
                ),
            ]
        ),

        html.Div(
            style={'display': 'flex', 'justifyContent': 'space-between'},

            # Left chart: Line chart with revenue over time by region
            children=[
                dcc.Graph(id='revenue-line'),

                # Right chart: Pie chart showing revenue share by region or by fraud status
                dcc.Graph(id='revenue-pie'),
            ]
        ),
    ]
)

# === CALLBACKS === #

@app.callback(
    Output('region-dropdown', 'value'),
    Input('sector-dropdown', 'value'),
    State('region-dropdown', 'value')
)
def update_region_based_on_sector(selected_sector, current_regions):
    # Chained callback example:
    # Simplify region options per sector by auto-resetting region selection if no overlap
    # Consulting prefers North America & Europe; Cloud prefers Asia & LATAM; Marketing all
    sector_region_map = {
        'Consulting': ['North America', 'Europe'],
        'Cloud Services': ['Asia', 'LATAM'],
        'Marketing': regions
    }
    allowed = sector_region_map[selected_sector]
    # Keep intersection of current_regions & allowed
    filtered = [r for r in current_regions if r in allowed]
    if not filtered:
        filtered = [allowed[0]]
    return filtered


@app.callback(
    Output('subregion-container', 'style'),
    Output('subregion-checklist', 'options'),
    Output('subregion-checklist', 'value'),
    Input('region-dropdown', 'value'),
    Input('fraud-toggle', 'on'),
    State('subregion-checklist', 'value')
)
def toggle_subregion_visibility(selected_regions, fraud_on, current_subregions):
    # Show subregion filter checklist only if fraud toggle ON and more than 1 region selected
    if not fraud_on or len(selected_regions) <= 1:
        # Hidden, clear checklist
        return {'display': 'none'}, [], []
    # Else show subregion filter checklist, options depend on selected regions
    # For this example, define static subregions per region
    subregions_all = {
        'North America': ['USA', 'Canada'],
        'Europe': ['UK', 'Germany', 'France'],
        'Asia': ['China', 'India', 'Japan'],
        'LATAM': ['Brazil', 'Argentina']
    }
    options = []
    for region in selected_regions:
        options += [{'label': f"{region} - {subr}", 'value': f"{region}:{subr}"} for subr in subregions_all[region]]
    # Keep selected values only if still valid
    valid_vals = [val for val in current_subregions if val in [opt['value'] for opt in options]]
    return {'display': 'block', 'marginBottom': '20px'}, options, valid_vals


@app.callback(
    Output('revenue-line', 'figure'),
    Output('revenue-pie', 'figure'),
    Input('sector-dropdown', 'value'),
    Input('region-dropdown', 'value'),
    Input('fraud-toggle', 'on'),
    Input('quarter-slider', 'value'),
    Input('subregion-checklist', 'value'),
)
def update_charts(sector, regions_selected, fraud_on, quarter_range, subregions_selected):
    # Filter data by sector
    dff = df[df['Sector'] == sector]

    # Filter by quarter range
    dff = dff[(dff['Quarter_Num'] >= quarter_range[0]) & (dff['Quarter_Num'] <= quarter_range[1])]

    # Filter by selected regions
    dff = dff[dff['Region'].isin(regions_selected)]

    # Conditional filtering: if fraud is ON, show only fraud-flagged rows
    if fraud_on:
        dff = dff[dff['FraudAlert'] == True]

        # Simulate subregion filtering on Revenue:
        # We'll fake subregions mapping by splitting Region revenue by subregions
        if subregions_selected:
            # Subregion codes: "Region:Subregion"
            # We simulate 50-50 split by subregion share randomly (fixed per run)
            # Aggregate revenue per Region * 0.5 per subregion in filtered list
            # But since we have no real subregion in dataset, we approximate visually.
            # Filter subregions to the relevant regions:
            selected_regions = list(set([subr.split(":")[0] for subr in subregions_selected]))
            # Build a new DataFrame with subregions splits
            rows = []
            for idx, row in dff.iterrows():
                base_rev = row['Revenue']
                # Count how many subregions exist for this region among selected
                subr_for_region = [s for s in subregions_selected if s.startswith(row['Region']+":")]
                if row['Region'] not in selected_regions:
                    continue  # skip rows outside selected regions (should not happen)
                if subr_for_region:
                    # Split revenue evenly among selected subregions for the row's region
                    rev_per_sub = base_rev / len(subr_for_region)
                    for s in subr_for_region:
                        rows.append({**row, 'Subregion': s.split(":")[1], 'Revenue': rev_per_sub})
                else:
                    # No subregion selected for this region, hide revenue
                    pass
            if rows:
                dff_sub = pd.DataFrame(rows)
            else:
                dff_sub = pd.DataFrame(columns=dff.columns.tolist() + ['Subregion'])
        else:
            dff_sub = dff.copy()
            dff_sub['Subregion'] = 'All'

    else:
        dff_sub = dff.copy()
        dff_sub['Subregion'] = 'All'

    # --- Build line chart ---
    # X axis: Quarter label; group by Region or Subregion if fraud filtering active with subregion filter
    if fraud_on and subregions_selected:
        group_col = 'Subregion'
        title_line = f"Revenue Trend for {sector} (Fraud Only) by Subregion"
    else:
        group_col = 'Region'
        title_line = f"Revenue Trend for {sector} by Region"

    dff_sub['Quarter_Label'] = dff_sub['Year'].astype(str) + " " + dff_sub['Quarter']

    fig_line = px.line(
        dff_sub,
        x='Quarter_Label',
        y='Revenue',
        color=group_col,
        markers=True,
        title=title_line,
        labels={'Revenue': 'Revenue (in $K)', 'Quarter_Label': 'Quarter'}
    )
    fig_line.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle= -45, margin={'t':50, 'b':100})

    # --- Build pie chart ---
    if fraud_on:
        # Pie by Region showing fraud revenue share (only fraud data)
        pie_title = "Revenue Share by Region (Fraud Only)"
        pie_df = dff[dff['Region'].isin(regions_selected)]
        pie_df_grouped = pie_df.groupby('Region')['Revenue'].sum().reset_index()
        fig_pie = px.pie(
            pie_df_grouped,
            names='Region',
            values='Revenue',
            title=pie_title
        )
    else:
        # Pie by Sector showing total revenue share by sector (all data, but filtered by selected regions for consistency)
        pie_title = "Revenue Share by Sector (All Data)"
        pie_df_grouped = df[df['Region'].isin(regions_selected)].groupby('Sector')['Revenue'].sum().reset_index()
        fig_pie = px.pie(
            pie_df_grouped,
            names='Sector',
            values='Revenue',
            title=pie_title
        )

    fig_pie.update_layout(margin={'t':50, 'b':0})

    return fig_line, fig_pie


# --------- Implement circular sync between fraud toggle and subregion checklist visibility ---------

# Idea: When fraud toggle OFF -> subregion is hidden and cleared.
# If user selects subregion checklist options and tries to turn fraud toggle OFF,
# automatically toggle fraud back ON (to avoid invalid states).
#
# This requires a circular callback pattern.
#
# We implement one circular pair here:
# - Callback A: toggles fraud toggle ON if subregion checklist has selections when fraud is OFF
# - Callback B: toggles subregion checklist visibility based on fraud toggle

# To avoid dash warnings for circular callbacks, we manage causality carefully inside callbacks.

@app.callback(
    Output('fraud-toggle', 'on'),
    Input('subregion-checklist', 'value'),
    State('fraud-toggle', 'on')
)
def enforce_fraud_toggle(subregion_vals, fraud_on):
    # If subregion values selected but fraud toggle is OFF, force fraud toggle ON
    if subregion_vals and not fraud_on:
        return True
    return fraud_on


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=False)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State, callback_context
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

np.random.seed(42)

# Generate dummy business data: Quarterly Revenue & Profit of 5 companies over 4 quarters (Q1-Q4, 2023)
companies = ['AlphaCorp', 'BetaInc', 'GammaLLC', 'DeltaPLC', 'EpsilonCo']
quarters = ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4']
regions = ['North America', 'Europe', 'Asia']  # For conditional visibility

# Base revenue means by company for realism
base_revenue = {
    'AlphaCorp': 200,
    'BetaInc': 150,
    'GammaLLC': 180,
    'DeltaPLC': 120,
    'EpsilonCo': 100
}
base_profit_margin = {
    'AlphaCorp': 0.25,
    'BetaInc': 0.18,
    'GammaLLC': 0.20,
    'DeltaPLC': 0.15,
    'EpsilonCo': 0.10
}

records = []
for comp in companies:
    for q_idx, q in enumerate(quarters):
        # Seasonality: Revenue increases from Q1 to Q3, dip Q4
        trend = 1 + 0.1 * q_idx - 0.05 * (q_idx == 3)  
        # Region-dependent scale
        for r in regions:
            revenue = base_revenue[comp] * trend * (1 + 0.1 * (regions.index(r) - 1))  
            revenue += np.random.normal(0, 10)
            profit_margin = base_profit_margin[comp]
            profit_margin += 0.02 * (regions.index(r) - 1)  # Higher margin in Asia
            profit = revenue * profit_margin + np.random.normal(0,5)
            records.append({
                'Company': comp,
                'Quarter': q,
                'Region': r,
                'Revenue': round(revenue,2),
                'Profit': round(profit,2)
            })

df = pd.DataFrame(records)

app = dash.Dash(__name__)

# --- Layout ---

app.layout = html.Div(
    style={'font-family': 'Arial, sans-serif', 'margin': '20px', 'max-width': '1400px'},
    children=[
        html.H2("Quarterly Revenue and Profit Analysis - 2023", style={'marginBottom': '15px'}),

        html.Div([
            html.Div([
                html.Label("Select Company:"),
                dcc.Dropdown(
                    id='company-dropdown',
                    options=[{'label': c, 'value': c} for c in companies],
                    value='AlphaCorp',
                    clearable=False,
                    style={'width': '240px'}
                )
            ], style={'display': 'inline-block', 'verticalAlign': 'top', 'marginRight': '40px'}),

            html.Div([
                html.Label("Select Region:"),
                dcc.Dropdown(
                    id='region-dropdown',
                    options=[{'label': r, 'value': r} for r in regions],
                    value='North America',
                    clearable=False,
                    style={'width': '180px'}
                )
            ], style={'display': 'inline-block', 'verticalAlign': 'top', 'marginRight': '40px'}),

            html.Div([
                html.Label("Select Metric:"),
                dcc.RadioItems(
                    id='metric-radio',
                    options=[
                        {'label': 'Revenue', 'value': 'Revenue'},
                        {'label': 'Profit', 'value': 'Profit'}
                    ],
                    value='Revenue',
                    labelStyle={'display': 'inline-block', 'marginRight': '20px'}
                )
            ], style={'display': 'inline-block', 'verticalAlign': 'top'}),
        ], style={'marginBottom': '20px'}),

        html.Div([
            # Conditional input container -- appears only if metric = Profit
            html.Div([
                html.Label("Profit Margin Filter (%):"),
                dcc.Input(
                    id='profit-margin-input',
                    type='number',
                    min=0,
                    max=50,
                    step=0.1,
                    value=20,
                    style={'width': '100px'}
                )
            ], id='profit-filter-container', style={'display': 'none', 'marginBottom': '20px'}),

            # Two synchronized dropdowns for Quarter selection: from and to quarter
            html.Div([
                html.Div([
                    html.Label("From Quarter:"),
                    dcc.Dropdown(
                        id='quarter-from',
                        options=[{'label': q, 'value': q} for q in quarters],
                        value='Q1',
                        clearable=False,
                        style={'width': '120px'}
                    )
                ], style={'display': 'inline-block', 'marginRight': '50px'}),

                html.Div([
                    html.Label("To Quarter:"),
                    dcc.Dropdown(
                        id='quarter-to',
                        options=[{'label': q, 'value': q} for q in quarters],
                        value='Q3',
                        clearable=False,
                        style={'width': '120px'}
                    )
                ], style={'display': 'inline-block'}),
            ], style={'marginBottom': '20px'}),
        ]),

        html.Div([
            dcc.Graph(
                id='bar-chart',
                config={'displayModeBar': False},
                style={'height': '500px'}
            )
        ])
    ]
)

# --- Callbacks ---

# 1. Conditional visibility for Profit Margin filter only if metric = 'Profit'
@app.callback(
    Output('profit-filter-container', 'style'),
    Input('metric-radio', 'value')
)
def toggle_profit_margin_input(metric):
    if metric == 'Profit':
        return {'display': 'block', 'marginBottom': '20px'}
    else:
        return {'display': 'none'}


# 2 & 4. Circular synchronization between 'quarter-from' and 'quarter-to'
# - quarter-from cannot be after quarter-to, quarter-to cannot be before quarter-from
# - Changing one updates the other if invalid.
#
# We use dash.callback_context to avoid infinite loops:
# If user changes quarter-from and it's > quarter-to, set quarter-to=quarter-from
# If user changes quarter-to and it's < quarter-from, set quarter-from=quarter-to

@app.callback(
    Output('quarter-to', 'value'),
    Input('quarter-from', 'value'),
    State('quarter-to', 'value')
)
def sync_quarter_to(q_from, q_to):
    q_from_idx = quarters.index(q_from)
    q_to_idx = quarters.index(q_to)
    if q_from_idx > q_to_idx:
        return q_from
    else:
        return q_to

@app.callback(
    Output('quarter-from', 'value'),
    Input('quarter-to', 'value'),
    State('quarter-from', 'value')
)
def sync_quarter_from(q_to, q_from):
    q_to_idx = quarters.index(q_to)
    q_from_idx = quarters.index(q_from)
    if q_to_idx < q_from_idx:
        return q_to
    else:
        return q_from


# 3. Complex chained output: Bar chart depends on company, region, metric, quarter range, and (if Profit) filtered by profit margin.
# - Uses State: profit margin input only matters if metric=Profit.

@app.callback(
    Output('bar-chart', 'figure'),
    Input('company-dropdown', 'value'),
    Input('region-dropdown', 'value'),
    Input('metric-radio', 'value'),
    Input('quarter-from', 'value'),
    Input('quarter-to', 'value'),
    State('profit-margin-input', 'value')
)
def update_bar_chart(company, region, metric, quarter_from, quarter_to, profit_margin):

    # Filter dataframe by company, region, quarter range
    q_from_idx = quarters.index(quarter_from)
    q_to_idx = quarters.index(quarter_to)

    filtered = df[
        (df['Company'] == company) &
        (df['Region'] == region) &
        (df['Quarter'].apply(lambda x: quarters.index(x) >= q_from_idx and quarters.index(x) <= q_to_idx))
    ]

    # If metric=Profit, further filter by profit margin threshold (Margin % = Profit / Revenue * 100)
    if metric == 'Profit' and profit_margin is not None:
        filtered = filtered.assign(
            MarginPercent = filtered['Profit'] / filtered['Revenue'] * 100
        )
        filtered = filtered[filtered['MarginPercent'] >= profit_margin]
        title = f"{company} Profit (Margin ≥ {profit_margin}%) in {region} from {quarter_from} to {quarter_to}"
    else:
        title = f"{company} {metric} in {region} from {quarter_from} to {quarter_to}"

    # Sort quarters ascending for proper x-axis.
    filtered = filtered.sort_values(by='Quarter', key=lambda x: x.apply(lambda v: quarters.index(v)))

    fig = px.bar(
        filtered,
        x='Quarter',
        y=metric,
        text=metric,
        title=title,
        labels={metric: metric + ' (Million USD)', 'Quarter': 'Quarter 2023'}
    )
    fig.update_traces(texttemplate='%{text:.2f}', textposition='outside')
    fig.update_layout(
        yaxis=dict(range=[0, max(filtered[metric].max() * 1.15, 10)]),  # some padding
        margin=dict(t=75, b=50, l=40, r=40),
        uniformtext_minsize=8, uniformtext_mode='hide'
    )

    return fig


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run_server(debug=True)

import plotly.express as px

# Fixed random seed for reproducibility
np.random.seed(42)

# ------- Data Generation (保持不变) -------
equipment_types = ["Compressor", "Turbine", "Pump", "Generator", "Boiler"]
time_points = pd.date_range("2024-01-01 00:00", periods=10, freq="H")


def generate_equipment_data(equip_type):
    base_temp = {

    }[equip_type]
    base_energy = {

    }[equip_type]

    temp_trend = base_temp + np.linspace(0, 15, 10)
    energy_trend = base_energy + 50 * np.sin(np.linspace(0, 3.14, 10))

    df = pd.DataFrame({
        "Timestamp": time_points,
        "Temperature": temp_trend,
        "Energy_Consumption": energy_trend,
    })
    return df


equipment_data = {et: generate_equipment_data(et) for et in equipment_types}

# ------- Dash App -------

app = dash.Dash(__name__)

app.layout = html.Div(

                            value="Compressor",

                        ),

                        dcc.Input(
                            id="load-input",
                            type="number",
                            min=50,

                        ),
                        html.Div(id="load-warning",
                                 style={"color": "firebrick", "fontWeight": "bold", "minHeight": "20px"}),
                        html.Label("Select Display Parameter:", style={"fontWeight": "bold"}),

                                {"label": "Energy Consumption", "value": "Energy_Consumption"},

                        ),
                    ],
                ),
                # Right visuals column
                html.Div(
                    style={"flex": "1", "marginLeft": "20px", "backgroundColor": "white",
                           "padding": "15px", "borderRadius": "6px", "boxShadow": "0 2px 4px #ccc",
                           "display": "flex", "flexDirection": "column"},
                    children=[
                        dcc.Graph(
                            id="line-chart",
                            style={"flex": "1", "minHeight": "400px"},
                            config={"displayModeBar": False},
                        ),
                        html.Div(
                            id="gauge-container",
                            style={"marginTop": "25px", "alignSelf": "center"},
                            children=[
                                daq.Gauge(
                                    id="temp-gauge",
                                    label="Current Temperature (°C)",
                                    min=40,
                                    max=130,
                                    value=0,
                                    style={"width": "300px", "height": "250px"},
                                )
                            ]
                        ),
                    ],
                ),
            ],
        ),
    ],
)


# --------- 修复后的 Callbacks ---------

@app.callback(
    Output("load-input", "value"),
    Output("equipment-dropdown", "value"),
    Output("load-warning", "children"),
    Input("load-input", "value"),
    Input("equipment-dropdown", "value"),
    State("load-input", "value"),
    prevent_initial_call=True,
)
def sync_load_equipment(load_val, equip_val, current_load):
    # 【修复重点 1】: 处理 None 值
    # 如果用户清空了输入框，load_val 会变为 None。
    # 我们将其设为 0，这样下面的 check (if load_val < 90) 就会触发并将值重置为 90。
    if load_val is None:
        load_val = 0

    changed_load = dash.callback_context.triggered and dash.callback_context.triggered[0][
        'prop_id'] == 'load-input.value'
    changed_equip = dash.callback_context.triggered and dash.callback_context.triggered[0][
        'prop_id'] == 'equipment-dropdown.value'

    warning = ""

    # If load input changes:
    if changed_load:
        # Enforce minimum load 90%
        if load_val < 90:
            corrected_load = 90
            warning = "Load increased to minimum stable 90%"
            load_val = corrected_load

        # If load > 110%, force equipment to Boiler
        if load_val > 110:
            equip_val = "Boiler"

    # If equipment input changes:
    elif changed_equip:
        # If equipment changed to Boiler but load < 110%, raise load to 110%
        if equip_val == "Boiler" and load_val < 110:
            load_val = 110
            warning = "Load raised to 110% for Boiler operation"

    return load_val, equip_val, warning


@app.callback(
    Output("line-chart", "figure"),
    Output("temp-gauge", "value"),
    Output("temp-gauge", "max"),
    Input("equipment-dropdown", "value"),
    Input("load-input", "value"),
    Input("display-radio", "value"),
)
def update_charts(equipment, load, display_param):
    # 【修复重点 2】: 防止 None 传给 numpy
    if load is None:
        return dash.no_update, dash.no_update, dash.no_update

    df = equipment_data[equipment].copy()

    # Operational load adjusts base param by a factor
    # Scale factor from load: 50% -> 0.8x, 150% -> 1.2x linearly
    # Clamp load input within [50, 150] to avoid surprises
    load = np.clip(load, 50, 150)
    scale_factor = 0.8 + (load - 50) / 100 * 0.4

    df[display_param] = df[display_param] * scale_factor

    # Prepare title depending on parameter
    yaxis_title = {
        "Energy_Consumption": "Energy Consumption (kW)",
        "Temperature": "Temperature (°C)"
    }[display_param]

    fig = px.line(
        df,
        x="Timestamp",
        y=display_param,
        title=f"{equipment} - {yaxis_title} over Time at {load}% Load",
        markers=True,
    )
    fig.update_layout(margin=dict(l=40, r=20, t=50, b=40))

    # Gauge shows current temperature scaled by load (always Temperature param)
    temp_val = df["Temperature"].iloc[-1]
    temp_val_scaled = temp_val * scale_factor
    max_temp = 130

    return fig, temp_val_scaled, max_temp


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run(debug=False)

import dash
from dash import dcc, html, Input, Output, State
import dash_bootstrap_components as dbc
import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Initialize the application
app = dash.Dash(__name__, external_stylesheets=[dbc.themes.FLATLY])
app.title = "AgriFuture Yield Optimizer"

# --- Dummy Data Generation Logic (Deterministic) ---
def generate_growth_data(region, irrigation, nitrogen_active, nitrogen_dose, fertilizer_p, fertilizer_k):
    """
    Generates deterministic growth data based on inputs.
    """
    days = np.arange(1, 16)  # 15 Days of data to keep it clean
    
    # Base Growth curve (Sigmoid-like)
    # Region 'north' is slower/lower yield, 'south' is faster/higher yield
    region_factor = 0.8 if region == 'north' else 1.2
    
    # Irrigation impact (Optimal at 60-80)
    # Penalize if too low or too high (Gaussian shape around 70)
    irrigation_factor = np.exp(-0.5 * ((irrigation - 70) / 30) ** 2)
    
    # Fertilizer impact
    n_boost = (nitrogen_dose / 50.0) * 0.3 if nitrogen_active else 0
    p_boost = 0.15 if 'P' in fertilizer_p else 0
    k_boost = 0.1 if 'K' in fertilizer_k else 0
    
    total_boost = 1 + n_boost + p_boost + k_boost
    
    biomass = []
    for d in days:
        # Logistic growth formula
        growth = (100 / (1 + np.exp(-0.5 * (d - 7.5)))) * region_factor * irrigation_factor * total_boost
        biomass.append(growth)
        
    df_area = pd.DataFrame({
        "Day": days,
        "Biomass (kg/ha)": biomass,
        "Region": [region.title()] * len(days)
    })
    
    # Nutrient Profile Data for Polar Chart
    # Values depend on inputs
    r_values = [
        20 + (nitrogen_dose if nitrogen_active else 0), # Nitrogen Uptake
        30 + (20 if 'P' in fertilizer_p else 0),       # Phosphorus Uptake
        25 + (15 if 'K' in fertilizer_k else 0),       # Potassium Uptake
        irrigation * 0.8                               # Water Saturation
    ]
    theta_values = ['Nitrogen', 'Phosphorus', 'Potassium', 'Water']
    
    df_polar = pd.DataFrame({
        "Nutrient": theta_values,
        "Absorption Level": r_values
    })
    
    return df_area, df_polar

# --- Layout ---
app.layout = dbc.Container([
    dbc.Row([
        dbc.Col(html.H2("AgriFuture: Crop Simulation Engine", className="text-primary my-3"), width=12)
    ]),
    
    dbc.Row([
        # Control Panel
        dbc.Col([
            dbc.Card([
                dbc.CardHeader("Configuration"),
                dbc.CardBody([
                    html.Label("1. Select Region (Base Climate):", className="fw-bold"),
                    dbc.Tabs(id="tab-region", active_tab="north", children=[
                        dbc.Tab(label="North Fields", tab_id="north"),
                        dbc.Tab(label="South Fields", tab_id="south"),
                    ], className="mb-3"),
                    
                    html.Label("2. Irrigation Control (mm):", className="fw-bold"),
                    dcc.Slider(
                        id="slider-irrigation",
                        min=0, max=100, step=10,
                        value=30,
                        marks={0: 'Dry', 50: 'Moderate', 100: 'Flood'},
                        tooltip={"placement": "bottom", "always_visible": True}
                    ),
                    html.Br(),
                    
                    html.Label("3. Fertilizers Applied:", className="fw-bold"),
                    dcc.Checklist(
                        id="checklist-fertilizers",
                        options=[
                            {'label': ' Nitrogen (N)', 'value': 'N'},
                            {'label': ' Phosphorus (P)', 'value': 'P'},
                            {'label': ' Potassium (K)', 'value': 'K'}
                        ],
                        value=['P'], # Default
                        labelStyle={'display': 'block', 'marginBottom': '5px'}
                    ),
                    
                    # Conditional Input: Only appears if 'N' is checked
                    html.Div(id="container-nitrogen-control", children=[
                        html.Hr(),
                        html.Label("Nitrogen Dosage (kg):", className="text-danger fw-bold"),
                        dcc.Slider(
                            id="slider-nitrogen",
                            min=0, max=100, step=10,
                            value=20,
                            marks={0: '0', 50: '50', 100: 'Max'},
                            tooltip={"placement": "bottom", "always_visible": True}
                        )
                    ], style={'display': 'none'}) # Hidden by default
                ])
            ], className="h-100")
        ], width=4, lg=3),
        
        # Visualization Panel
        dbc.Col([
            dbc.Row([
                dbc.Col([
                    dbc.Card([
                        dbc.CardHeader("Projected Biomass Growth (15 Days)"),
                        dbc.CardBody(dcc.Graph(id="chart-area", style={'height': '350px'}))
                    ], className="mb-3")
                ], width=12)
            ]),
            dbc.Row([
                dbc.Col([
                    dbc.Card([
                        dbc.CardHeader("Nutrient Absorption Profile"),
                        dbc.CardBody(dcc.Graph(id="chart-polar", style={'height': '300px'}))
                    ])
                ], width=12)
            ])
        ], width=8, lg=9)
    ])
], fluid=True, style={'padding': '20px', 'maxHeight': '100vh'})


# --- Callbacks ---

# 1. Chained Callback: Region Tab -> Irrigation Slider Default/Range
# Changing the region implies different soil needs, so we adjust the recommended irrigation slider.
@app.callback(
    [Output("slider-irrigation", "value"),
     Output("slider-irrigation", "marks")],
    Input("tab-region", "active_tab")
)
def update_irrigation_settings(active_tab):
    if active_tab == "south":
        # South is hotter, default irrigation should be higher
        return 70, {0: 'Dry', 70: 'Ideal', 100: 'Flood'}
    else:
        # North is wetter/cooler, default irrigation lower
        return 30, {0: 'Dry', 30: 'Ideal', 100: 'Flood'}

# 2. Conditional Visibility: Checklist -> Nitrogen Slider Container
@app.callback(
    Output("container-nitrogen-control", "style"),
    Input("checklist-fertilizers", "value")
)
def toggle_nitrogen_slider(selected_fertilizers):
    if 'N' in selected_fertilizers:
        return {'display': 'block'}
    return {'display': 'none'}

# 3. Main Visualization Callback: All Inputs -> Charts
@app.callback(
    [Output("chart-area", "figure"),
     Output("chart-polar", "figure")],
    [Input("tab-region", "active_tab"),
     Input("slider-irrigation", "value"),
     Input("checklist-fertilizers", "value"),
     Input("slider-nitrogen", "value")]
)
def update_charts(region, irrigation, fertilizers, nitrogen_dose):
    # Determine flags
    n_active = 'N' in fertilizers
    
    # Generate Data
    df_area, df_polar = generate_growth_data(
        region, irrigation, n_active, nitrogen_dose, fertilizers, fertilizers
    )
    
    # Plot Area Chart
    fig_area = px.area(
        df_area, x="Day", y="Biomass (kg/ha)", 
        title=f"Growth Projection - {region.title()} Region",
        markers=True,
        template="plotly_white"
    )
    fig_area.update_layout(margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=40, b=20), hovermode="x unified")
    fig_area.update_traces(line_color="#2ecc71", fillcolor="rgba(46, 204, 113, 0.3)")
    
    # Plot Polar Chart
    fig_polar = px.bar_polar(
        df_polar, r="Absorption Level", theta="Nutrient",
        title="Current Nutrient Absorption",
        template="plotly_white",
        color="Nutrient",
        color_discrete_map={
            'Nitrogen': '#e74c3c', 'Phosphorus': '#f39c12', 
            'Potassium': '#8e44ad', 'Water': '#3498db'
        }
    )
    fig_polar.update_layout(margin=dict(l=40, r=40, t=40, b=20), showlegend=True)
    
    return fig_area, fig_polar

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run_server(debug=True)
